topics.title: Canada gets a Eurovision debut
topics.summary: Canada will compete in Eurovision for the first time in 2027, with CBC set to explain later how the Canadian entry will be chosen.
topics.discussionPrompt: Canada at Eurovision sounds wonderfully odd. What kind of Canadian song or performer do you think would play best on that huge international stage?

topics.title: US and Iran focus on Hormuz talks
topics.summary: U.S. and Iranian negotiators wrapped technical talks in Doha focused on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and the possible unfreezing of Iranian funds.
topics.discussionPrompt: The Strait of Hormuz is far away, but it can affect oil prices and shipping. Do global chokepoints like that make the world feel more connected or more fragile?

topics.title: France battles heat-driven wildfires
topics.summary: Firefighters in southern France battled several wind-fanned wildfires after recent extreme heat, with the largest blaze burning about 900 hectares near Spain.
topics.discussionPrompt: Heatwaves are becoming part of summer planning in more places. How would you change vacations, festivals or daily routines around wildfire risk?

topics.title: NASA satellite rescue hits delay
topics.summary: A rescue mission for NASA's Swift Observatory was delayed when a Pegasus rocket was not released from its carrier aircraft; no new launch date has been set.
topics.discussionPrompt: A robotic rescue mission for an aging space telescope feels very sci-fi. Should agencies spend more to save old spacecraft, or focus on launching new ones?

topics.title: US advances in World Cup drama
topics.summary: The U.S. beat Bosnia 2-0 in a World Cup knockout match, but scorer Folarin Balogun was sent off and will miss the last-16 match against Belgium.
topics.discussionPrompt: One red card can change a team's whole tournament. Is that part of soccer's drama, or does it sometimes feel too harsh?
